_comment: Benchmark scores mapped to the 1-5 maturity scale used by this assessment. Industry averages and top-quartile (75th percentile) figures are derived from published annual research reports listed in the 'sources' array.

sources.description: 861 respondents representing ~$69B cloud spend. Crawl 34%, Walk 51%, Run 14%. AI cost management nearly doubled to 63% of practices managing AI spend.
966 respondents, $83B+ cloud spend. 98% now managing AI costs (up from 63% in 2025). SaaS management at 90%. FOCUS standard adoption at 85% for $100M+ orgs.
Annual FinOps maturity survey of 1,400+ practitioners. 21% Crawl, 56% Walk, 23% Run stage distribution. AI cost management at 31% of practices.
78% of organizations regularly use AI in at least one function. 72% use generative AI (up from 33% in 2024). Only ~6% are AI high performers with measurable EBIT impact. 23% scaling AI agents.
88% of organizations regularly use AI (up from 78% in March). AI adoption amplifies both strengths and dysfunctions — high performers 3x more likely to be scaling AI agents.
Global survey of 1,300+ executives on AI adoption maturity. Only 11% report mature AI implementation.

90% of organizations now have platform engineering capabilities. 90% use AI in their work. Individual AI gains (21% task completion increase) do not yet translate to org-level delivery improvement. Added rework rate as 5th key metric.
39,000+ respondents. Elite 19%, High 22%, Medium 34%, Low 25%. Low performers grew from 17% (2023) to 25% — a significant regression. Elite performers 182x more frequent deployments than Low.
44% have implemented unified observability platforms; 29% actively transitioning. 53% say observability falls short of needs. 41% using OpenTelemetry in production. Average 8 tools per org.

sources.report: Lack of AI-Ready Data Puts AI Projects at Risk
sources.description: 248 data management leaders surveyed. 63% lack right data management practices for AI. 53% have implemented data observability tools. Only 15% have mature data governance. 59% do not measure data quality.

sources.report: Gartner Survey: AI Maturity and Project Longevity
sources.description: n=432. 45% of high-AI-maturity organizations keep AI initiatives in production 3+ years vs. 20% in low-maturity orgs. 91% of high-maturity orgs have dedicated AI leadership.

sources.report: State of Platform Engineering in the Age of AI
sources.description: n=1,000. 86% have started implementing platform engineering. 54% of advanced-maturity orgs report significant innovation gains. 13% have achieved optimized cross-functional ecosystems (highest tier).
n=301. 41% prioritize SRE as top priority. Median 30% of time spent on toil (up from 25% — first rise in 5 years). Only 37% regularly practice chaos engineering. 51% say observability falls short.
10,000+ customers analyzed. GenAI usage 75% (up from 55% in 2023). Only 22% have architecture that can effectively support AI workloads. Vector databases grew 377% YoY.

sources.report: BI and AI Maturity Model Assessment 2025
sources.description: Updated BI and AI maturity framework covering five dimensions. Five stages: Nascent, Early, Established, Mature, Advanced/Visionary. Allows benchmarking against industry peers.
TDWI annual data management maturity benchmark covering data quality, governance, and integration.
Average 1 data quality issue per 10 tables/year. 68% take 4+ hours to detect incidents. 74% of orgs: business stakeholders still identify issues first. 67 monthly data incidents per org on average.
